lede: A look at May 13 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Romain Esse and Mark Stone, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Soma mine disaster
events.desc: An explosion at an underground coal mine in southwest Turkey kills 301 miners.

events.title: Cadereyta Jiménez massacre
events.desc: Forty-nine dismembered bodies are discovered by Mexican authorities on Mexican Federal Highway 40.

events.title: 2011 Charsadda bombing
events.desc: Two bombs explode in the Charsadda District of Pakistan killing 98 people and wounding 140 others.

events.title: Enschede fireworks disaster
events.desc: A fireworks storage depot explodes in a residential neighborhood in Enschede, Netherlands, killing 23 people and injuring 950 others.

events.title: Alison Hargreaves
events.desc: Alison Hargreaves, a 33-year-old British mother, becomes the first woman to ascend Everest without oxygen or the help of sherpas.

events.title: Li Hongzhi
events.desc: Li Hongzhi gives the first public lecture on Falun Gong in Changchun, People's Republic of China.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: A car bombing outside a crowded pub in Belfast sparks a two-day gun battle involving the Provisional IRA, Ulster Volunteer Force and British Army. Seven people are killed and over 66 injured.

events.title: Zakir Husain
events.desc: Dr. Zakir Husain becomes the third President of India. He is the first Muslim President of the Indian Union. He holds this position until August 24, 1969.

events.title: Ben Carlin
events.desc: Ben Carlin becomes the first (and only) person to circumnavigate the world by amphibious vehicle, having travelled over 17,000 kilometres (11,000 mi) by sea and 62,000 kilometres (39,000 mi) by land during a ten-year journey.

events.title: 1909 Giro d'Italia
events.desc: The first edition of the Giro d'Italia, a long-distance multiple-stage bicycle race, begins in Milan; the Italian cyclist Luigi Ganna was the eventual winner.

births.name: Darryl Sydor
births.line: Canadian ice hockey player and coach

deaths.text: Kit Bond, American lawyer and politician, 47th Governor of Missouri
J. F. Coleman, American soldier and pilot
Wallace McCain, Canadian businessman, co-founded McCain Foods
Hao Wang (academic), Chinese-American logician, philosopher, and mathematician

observances.text: Christian feast day: Our Lady of Fátima
Christian feast day: John the Silent (Roman Catholic)
Christian feast day: Julian of Norwich (Roman Catholic)
